steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best describes the overall structure of the text. Map out the three moves the passage makes before matching to the choices.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It argues for a new approach to scientific research, comments on the public's opinion about the approach, and then describes how that approach was applied in a certain study.: The passage lists concrete benefits, not public opinion. ✗
  • Choice B — It introduces the topic of a scientific study, describes the study's importance, and then presents the study's results.: The Nadia Flores butterfly study is an example at the end, not the primary focus. ✗
  • Choice C — It describes the development of a type of scientific collaboration, shows how that type of collaboration has been used in a particular field of study, and then suggests future collaborative projects.: No development history or future suggestions appear in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — It identifies a particular approach to research, lists some benefits of that approach, and then mentions a study in which that approach was used.: Matches all three moves: community science is defined and called effective, benefits are listed, and the Alabama butterfly study is cited as an example. ✓
D is correct.
